modes.desc: Fastest across zones 1–2. 11 lines. Daily cap £8.90.
One-hour Hopper: unlimited buses in 60 min for one fare.
New east-west line. Heathrow to Bond St in 32 min.
Uber Boat by Thames Clippers. Views + no traffic.
Central London is 3 km wide. Everything is walkable.
Cheaper than black cab off-peak. Split fares in-app.

oysterTips.title: Contactless bank card
oysterTips.desc: Just tap the yellow reader. Daily cap £8.90 · weekly cap £44.70. Uses your bank's FX rate — usually best.

oysterTips.title: Apple / Google Pay
oysterTips.desc: Same as contactless. Use the same card each journey or your fares won't cap properly.

oysterTips.title: Oyster (visitor)
oysterTips.desc: Only useful if your card charges FX fees. £7 deposit + top up. Refund at any station machine.

oysterTips.title: Under 11s free
oysterTips.desc: Kids travel free on Tube, DLR, bus and Elizabeth Line with an adult. No paperwork needed.

transport.exit6Tip: Use exit 6 at Westminster tube — you emerge with Big Ben framed directly in front of you. Every other exit spits you into traffic. Your first photo of London is worth planning for.

maps.caption: A schematic map — for exact directions, scan any QR code in this guide.

warnings.title: Airport taxis
warnings.desc: Never take a black cab from a random touting driver at LHR. Elizabeth Line + hotel walk = £11.50. A booked Uber from arrivals: £45–£60.

warnings.title: Currency exchange near Westminster
warnings.desc: Anywhere with a sandwich-board and no fees advertised is 4–8% worse than your bank card. Use your contactless card everywhere instead.

warnings.title: Fake ticket sellers
warnings.desc: London Eye, Eye of London, and street-corner ticket sellers around Piccadilly are common scams. Only buy via the official websites in this guide.

warnings.title: Restaurants opposite Big Ben
warnings.desc: £25 for a mediocre fish and chips. Walk 8 minutes to Regency Café or Poppie's for the real thing at half the price.

warnings.title: Souvenirs on Westminster Bridge
warnings.desc: £15 fridge magnets. Buy the same thing at Camden Market or Portobello Road for £4.

warnings.title: Illegal pedicabs
warnings.desc: £40 for a 200-metre ride. Uncapped, unregulated. Just walk or Uber.

warnings.title: SIM cards at airport kiosks
warnings.desc: £40 for what should cost £10. Use eSIM apps like Airalo or buy from any Boots/EE shop in town.

family.tip: Children under 11 travel free on tube, bus, DLR and Elizabeth Line when with a fare-paying adult. No paperwork needed. 11–15 needs a Zip Oyster photocard if staying long-term.

emergency.lostPassport: Report to the nearest police station (dial 101). Contact your embassy — they can issue an emergency travel document within 24–48 h. Take photos of your passport and store them in cloud storage before you fly.

numbers.desc: UK Foreign Office · lost passport line

phrases.use: Cards work everywhere; still ask if uncertain
Ask for the bill · never wait for it
Means thank you AND goodbye · use freely

etiquette.desc: Escalators. Always. Left is for walking. Break this rule and you will be tutted at.
Britons queue for buses, coffee, taxis and toilets. Never skip. Ever.
And mind it out loud. That announcement is a national treasure.
London's rush hour is a monastic silence. Save conversation for the pub.
And 'sorry'. Twenty times a day. It is the entire operating system.

etiquette.rule: Keep your phone in your pocket
etiquette.desc: On busy streets. Phone theft by moped is real. Use pockets or bags with zips.
Then left, then right again. Britain drives on the left.

hiddenGems.desc: Holland Park · koi pond in autumn
Best skyline · 15 min walk from Camden
Sculptural noses hidden on 7 walls · a self-guided treasure hunt

vatTips.note: VAT is 20% on most goods. As of 2021 the UK ended in-store VAT refunds for tourists. However, many luxury boutiques ship your purchase to your home address VAT-free — always ask at the till.
vatTips.saleSeasons: Boxing Day (26 Dec) → mid-January · End of June to end of July · Selfridges & Harrods run private-sale evenings — sign up to their newsletters.
vatTips.shippingTip: For any single purchase over £600 (Louis Vuitton, Burberry, Rolex etc.), always ask about VAT-free direct shipping to your home country. You save 20% and skip customs at the airport.

districts.desc: Luxury flagships · Louis Vuitton, Chanel, Hermès
Mass market · Selfridges, John Lewis, Zara
Under one roof · easy on rainy days · Elizabeth Line
Outlet 1 h from Marylebone by train · up to 60% off

souvenirs: a Twinings gift box from Strand

budget.tip: No matter how many tubes and buses you take, London's contactless daily cap is £8.90 in zones 1–2. Never buy a paper ticket — you would only spend more.
